frame_analyses.description: Initial view of training setup with yellow Penrose drain secured by black tape marks, instruments visible at bottom
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear view of target marks on drain for suture placement

frame_analyses.description: Right instrument grasping suture, approaching first mark on Penrose drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good instrument control, suture being positioned for first penetration

frame_analyses.description: Suture being passed through drain near first mark, left instrument assisting
Suture visible passing through drain, working toward second mark
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Thread visible on both sides of drain

frame_analyses.description: Both suture ends visible, preparing for extracorporeal knot formation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture placement appears complete, ready for knot tying phase

frame_analyses.description: Instruments positioned for extracorporeal knot tying, suture ends being manipulated
Right instrument appears to be pushing knot down toward drain surface
Continued knot advancement, instruments working to secure knot position
Final positioning and preparation for suture trimming
Task completed, instruments withdrawn, knot visible on drain surface
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final result visible with knot secured

penalties.description: Unable to precisely measure deviation from marks due to camera angle and instrument obstruction

confidence_rationale: Camera angle and instrument positioning frequently obscured precise visualization of suture entry points relative to marks. Knot formation and completion were well-visualized. Conservative penalty estimate applied due to limited visual confirmation.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter deviation from marks - instruments and camera angle obscured critical views
Precise suture entry angles - oblique viewing angle throughout procedure
technique_summary: Competent intermediate performance with methodical approach. Extended time in knot pushing phase (64s) suggests room for efficiency improvement. Overall technique appeared sound with proper extracorporeal knot formation.
improvement_suggestions: Reduce knot pushing time from 64s to under 40s for expert-level performance
Practice precise suture placement to minimize deviation penalties
Work on overall efficiency to achieve sub-180s completion time
